seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Chesney Vale and Chetwynd. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Chesney Vale vs Chetwynd: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chesney Vale and Chetwynd.
heading: Chesney Vale vs Chetwynd — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chesney Vale, Victoria and Chetwynd, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chesney Vale has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Chetwynd (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chesney Vale or Chetwynd safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chesney Vale has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Chetwynd scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chesney Vale scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Chetwynd scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chesney Vale or Chetwynd?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chesney Vale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
